Eq

Implementors

In script::dom::bindings::import::module::cmp

?

Trait script::dom::bindings::import::module::cmp::Eq

1.0.0 · source · 
pub trait Eq: PartialEq<Self> { }
Expand description

Trait for equality comparisons which are equivalence relations.

This means, that in addition to a == b and a != b being strict inverses, the equality must be (for all a, b and c):

  • reflexive: a == a;
  • symmetric: a == b implies b == a; and
  • transitive: a == b and b == c implies a == c.

This property cannot be checked by the compiler, and therefore Eq implies PartialEq, and has no extra methods.

Violating this property is a logic error. The behavior resulting from a logic error is not specified, but users of the trait must ensure that such logic errors do not result in undefined behavior. This means that unsafe code must not rely on the correctness of these methods.

Derivable

This trait can be used with #[derive]. When derived, because Eq has no extra methods, it is only informing the compiler that this is an equivalence relation rather than a partial equivalence relation. Note that the derive strategy requires all fields are Eq, which isn’t always desired.

How can I implement Eq?

If you cannot use the derive strategy, specify that your type implements Eq, which has no methods:

enum BookFormat { Paperback, Hardback, Ebook }
struct Book {
    isbn: i32,
    format: BookFormat,
}
impl PartialEq for Book {
    fn eq(&self, other: &Self) -> bool {
        self.isbn == other.isbn
    }
}
impl Eq for Book {}

impl Eq for FontTemplateDescriptor

FontTemplateDescriptor contains floats, which are not Eq because of NaN. However, we know they will never be NaN, so we can manually implement Eq.

impl Eq for ron::value::Float

Equality comparison In order to be able to use Float as a mapping key, NaN floating values wrapped in Float are equal to each other. It is not the case for underlying f64 values itself.
